content.text:
IS Urbanisation and Strategic Alignment
Semester:
3  |
Type:
UEF31  |
VHS:
63h  |
Weekly hours:
C: 3h / TD: 1h30  |
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
5
Teaching Objectives
The main objective is to provide students with the knowledge and skills needed to lead and manage an urbanisation project, ensuring coherence and alignment between the information system and the strategic objectives of the enterprise.
Recommended prior knowledge:
Information systems.
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Introduction to IS Urbanisation
Definition and key concepts of IS urbanisation (zone, neighbourhood, block)
Objectives and benefits (rationalisation, strategic alignment, flexibility, agility, etc.)
Different levels of urbanisation (business, functional, application, technical)
Urbanisation Framework and Principles
Definition of the IS reference framework (business, functional, application, technical)
IS urban planning plans and components (target architecture, urban planning rules, cartographies)
Urbanisation principles: modularity, reuse, standardisation, etc.
Data and service repository management
Enterprise Architecture and Urbanisation Frameworks
Enterprise architecture principles and models
Urbanisation frameworks (e.g. TOGAF)
IS cartographies and business process modelling
Identification of IS components (applications, data, infrastructure)
IS Strategic Alignment
Understanding IS/Business strategic alignment
Impact of enterprise strategy on IS
IS alignment with business objectives
Development of an IS urbanisation roadmap
Alignment monitoring and evaluation
IS Governance and Strategic Alignment
Setting up governance to lead and control IS urbanisation
Urbanisation risk management
Stakeholder communication and change management
Urbanisation project steering and monitoring
Urbanisation and Technologies
Impact of Cloud, SOA, and microservices architectures on urbanisation
IS interoperability and integration
Data management in an urbanisation context
Security and resilience of the urbanised IS
IS Urbanisation Approach
Identification and analysis of existing state (IS cartography)
Definition of the IS target architecture
Implementation of urban planning rules and IS governance
Urbanisation approaches: Veronique Levasseur approach, Christophe Longépé approach, "Think Service" approach by Yann Le Tanou
Assessment
In-person semester exam (60%)
Continuous assessment (CC) (40%): continuous tests, personal work
References
C. Longépé, R. Colletti, G. Balantzian (2009).
Le projet d'urbanisation du SI : Cas concret d'architecture d'entreprise
. Editions Dunod.
Sassoon Jacques (1998).
Urbanisation des systèmes d'information
. Editions Hermes.
Yann le Tanou (2006).
Urbanisation Orienté-Service: L'approche 'think service'
. Valtech Technologie Consulting.
